name: Montañas Vacías and Cuervo River Source Loop
name_original: Parque Natural Serranía de Cuenca – circular desde Tragacete hasta el nacimiento del río Cuervo por Montañas Vacías
description: Tackle a difficult 25.9-mile mountain bike loop from Tragacete to the Nacimiento del Río Cuervo in Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Tragacete offers diverse terrain within the Cuenca Mountain Range, characterized by deep valleys, impressive hills like Mogorrita, and a network of forest and mountain paths. The region is shaped by the Júcar riverbed and its proximity to the sources of the Júcar and Cuervo rivers, featuring notable waterfalls and varied landscapes. These natural features, combined with the wider Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, provide a range of mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Tragacete The most popular mountain bike trail is Vuelta al Mogorrita desde Tragacete — Serranía de Cuenca, a 25.3 miles (40.7 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete, circling the notable Mogorrita hill. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cascada del Molino desde Tragacete – circular por el Parque Natural del Alto Tajo, a difficult 30.3 miles (48.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and leads to the scenic Cascada del Molino. Local mountain bikers also love the Montañas Vacías and Cuervo River Source Loop, a 25.9 miles (41.7 km) trail leading through the Montañas Vacías and to the Source of the Cuervo River, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Mountain biking around Tragacete is defined by river valleys, forest paths, and mountain trails, with routes offering considerable elevation gain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Tragacete are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 280 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Tragacete's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Tragacete?
A: Tragacete offers a good selection of mountain biking opportunities, with over 25 trails available. These routes range from moderate to difficult, providing options for various skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Tragacete?
A: While there are no trails explicitly rated as 'easy' in our data, 16 of the routes are classified as 'moderate'. These trails, often consisting of forest paths and mountain trails, could be suitable for families or beginners with some prior mountain biking experience. The region's diverse terrain allows for routes of varying difficulty.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Tragacete?
A: Yes, Tragacete is an excellent base for circular mountain bike routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1376219581/vuelta-al-mogorrita-desde-tragacete-serrania-de-cuenca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Mogorrita desde Tragacete — Serranía de Cuenca&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop that circles the notable Mogorrita hill. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016902034/cascada-del-molino-desde-tragacete-circular-por-el-parque-natural-del-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Molino desde Tragacete – circular por el Parque Natural del Alto Tajo&lt;/a&gt;, which leads to the scenic Molino Waterfall.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that pass by waterfalls or natural monuments?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its stunning natural features. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016925401/montanas-vacias-and-cuervo-river-source-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montañas Vacías and Cuervo River Source Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you to the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuervo River&lt;/a&gt;, famous for its stunning waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016902034/cascada-del-molino-desde-tragacete-circular-por-el-parque-natural-del-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Molino desde Tragacete – circular por el Parque Natural del Alto Tajo&lt;/a&gt; also leads to the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5400567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Tragacete?
A: Mountain biking around Tragacete is characterized by diverse terrain within the Cuenca Mountain Range. You'll encounter deep valleys, impressive hills, and a network of forest paths and mountain trails. Routes often involve considerable elevation gain and can include technical sections, requiring good mountain fitness and appropriate bikes with suspension.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Tragacete?
A: The trails around Tragacete are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Mountain bikers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Serranía de Cuenca, and the opportunities to explore significant natural landmarks like river sources and waterfalls.

Q: Are there any specific regulations for mountain biking in the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park?
A: The trails around Tragacete are within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. While specific permits for mountain biking are generally not required for established trails, it's always advisable to stay on marked paths to protect the natural environment. For detailed regulations, especially concerning protected areas, it's best to consult local park authorities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Tragacete?
A: Many trails within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park are dog-friendly, but it's crucial to keep your dog under control, preferably on a leash, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always check specific trail regulations, as some sensitive areas might have restrictions.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Tragacete?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Tragacete, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, especially at midday, while winters can bring snow and colder temperatures, making some higher elevation trails challenging or inaccessible.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Tragacete offers several challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016925401/montanas-vacias-and-cuervo-river-source-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montañas Vacías and Cuervo River Source Loop&lt;/a&gt; is rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation changes and technical sections. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016902034/cascada-del-molino-desde-tragacete-circular-por-el-parque-natural-del-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Molino desde Tragacete – circular por el Parque Natural del Alto Tajo&lt;/a&gt;, known for its considerable elevation gain.

Q: What are some other interesting places to explore near the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the trails, the region boasts several points of interest. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuervo River&lt;/a&gt; with its stunning waterfalls, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5400567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. For those interested in local history or seeking shelter, there are also various huts and shelters like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio de la Alconera&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Lorenzo Hermitage and Shelter&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: The Cuervo River Spring is a dream place in the Serranía de Cuenca, Spain. This Natural Monument houses a main waterfall with a height of more than 20 meters, leaving those who contemplate it breathless. Here, the Cuervo River springs from a spring of travertine origin, flowing over limestone rock covered with moss, forming beautiful waterfalls called chorreras.

The Montañas Vacías route is a circular itinerary popular among lovers of bikepacking and cycling through remote places. It is a circular itinerary that runs through the most uninhabited area of Spain known as the "Spanish Lapland" although the places you will visit are truly wonderful!
This stretch of just over 10 kilometers connects the towns of Tragacete with Vega del Codorno through a wonderful forest that will captivate you.

faqs.question: What is the estimated riding time for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain bike route is approximately 41.7 km long with nearly 990 meters of elevation gain. It typically takes around 4 hours and 9 minutes of active riding time to complete, but this can vary significantly based on your fitness level and technical skill.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is considered difficult and is not recommended for beginners. It demands a very good physical condition and technical proficiency, with sections rated STS S3-S6, meaning some parts may require dismounting and pushing your bike. Low gearing is highly recommended.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park in Tragacete?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Tragacete, a convenient and welcoming town. While specific parking locations aren't detailed, Tragacete offers tourist services, including accommodation and restaurants, suggesting ample parking options for visitors. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding. You'll encounter a mix of well-maintained tracks and forest roads, often shared with walking routes. Be prepared for technical segments and some challenging cobbled climbs, particularly on the ascent to the Nacimiento del Río Cuervo. Gravel bike riders might encounter short hike-a-bike sections.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the stunning Source and waterfalls of the Cuervo River, a Natural Monument famous for its beautiful cascades over moss-covered rock formations. The route also traverses a section of the broader Montañas Vacías network, known for its remote and untouched wilderness.

faqs.question: What wildlife or natural features can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: You'll ride through the beautiful landscapes of the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, characterized by extensive pine forests, deep canyons, and rivers with crystalline waters. Keep an eye out for impressive limestone formations. Wildlife sightings are common and may include deer, wild boar, ibex, hares, foxes, and various bird species like vultures.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route is generally during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, while winter may bring snow and colder conditions, making some sections impassable or more challenging.

faqs.question: Is this route in the Parque Natural Serranía de Cuenca dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Parque Natural Serranía de Cuenca generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the presence of wild boar and other animals. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Parque Natural Serranía de Cuenca?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Parque Natural Serranía de Cuenca for activities like mountain biking. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked trails, and be mindful of protected areas, especially around sensitive natural monuments like the Nacimiento del Río Cuervo.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. Notably, it covers about 39% of the 'Puente Martinete-Tragacete' trail and 35% of the 'MontañasVacías' network. You'll also briefly encounter parts of 'CIMA CU06] La Vega - Serracín', 'Ruta de San Blas, San Felipe y el río Cuervo', and 'Camino de las Acebeas', among others.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for tackling this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and technical sections, particularly the cobbled climbs and potential hike-a-bike segments, consider starting early in the day. This allows ample time to complete the ride without rushing and to fully enjoy the Nacimiento del Río Cuervo, which can get busy later in the day. Ensure your bike is equipped with low gearing to manage the significant elevation gain effectively.

waypoints.name: Source and waterfalls of the Cuervo River
